evidence_quotes.quote: Video games are often sold nowadays, without exception, mainly on computers as a digital licence.
Under current legislation, a publisher can often simply ‘switch off’ these games as it sees fit.

paragraphs.text: I have been a fan of any kind of video games since I was a child. Most of my friends are also supporters of video games since childhood days. As we are a little older, we still have the chance to play these games today, because we have them as a physical copy and there was no online obligation at that time. Unfortunately, children and adolescents will no longer have this possibility in the future. Video games are often sold nowadays, without exception, mainly on computers as a digital licence. Under current legislation, a publisher can often simply ‘switch off’ these games as it sees fit. I would therefore ask you to take this into account in this law and to introduce rules accordingly. I really want the video games of recent years to remain available in 20+ years, so that today’s young people can play them again with nostalgia. I also think that video games are similar to films and should not be lost.
